{"society_power": 6.7, "state_power": 7.3, "society_change": 0.7, "state_change": -0.2, "key_event": "Salt March / Civil Disobedience and Purna Swaraj declaration", "reasoning": "Mass civil disobedience, Congress institutionalization, and the state's forced negotiation (Gandhi-Irwin Pact) surged society's mobilizing power, though communal and caste fractures capped the gain. The colonial state retained strong coercive capacity but saw legitimacy corroded and had to concede bargaining status, yielding a slight net decline."}
